items.wo_reason: family_support_guide incorporates Nigerian food examples (mashed yam, soft fufu, stews, rice) into the dysphagia diet section, notes Sunday morning scheduling preference, and specifies male aide preference. hha_visit_guide and care_profile both note these preferences as actionable scheduling/care decisions. Cultural background is integrated into concrete recommendations rather than only mentioned demographically.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully with all verifier checks passing.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, clinically informed care support packages that pass all six evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills demonstrates meaningfully superior quality across several dimensions: (1) It produced 6 structured files with a README index versus 4 files, showing better professional organization. (2) The stair railing hazard analysis in wi_skills explicitly identified the right-side-only railing as a specific risk when descending with the affected left side exposed — a stroke-specific clinical insight not present in wo_skills. (3) The communication section in file 01 included specific example phrasings ('Mr. Okafor, would you like to wash up before breakfast or after?') that demonstrate deeper aphasia-adapted communication training. (4) File 05 (coordination notes) in wi_skills added a clinical clarification note about a handedness discrepancy between the referral and family interview — demonstrating cross-source synthesis that wo_skills did not perform. (5) The interdisciplinary referral section in wi_skills was more explicitly structured with escalation triggers and scope-of-practice boundaries. Overall, wi_skills produced a more clinically rigorous, professionally structured package that more fully leverages HHA professional frameworks, while wo_skills produced a competent but slightly less structured output. wi_skills is the more effective run.
